Fall (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $7,240,521Details
International Box Office $14,531,046
Worldwide Box Office $21,771,567
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,450,638
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $925,434
Total $2,376,072
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For best friends Becky and Hunter, life is all about conquering fears and pushing limits. But after they climb 2,000 feet to the top of a remote, abandoned radio tower, they find themselves stranded with no way down. Now Becky and Hunter’s expert climbing skills will be put to the ultimate test as they desperately fight to survive the elements, a lack of supplies, and vertigo-inducing heights.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,512,145 (34.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.88 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:1,548 opening theaters/1,548 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $8,223,804

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 12th, 2022 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: August 11th, 2022 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
August 25th, 2022 (Limited) (New Zealand)
September 1st, 2022 (Wide), released as Vértigo (Mexico)
September 2nd, 2022 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 2nd, 2022 (Limited) (Netherlands)
Video Release: September 27th, 2022 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for bloody images, intense peril, and strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2737 (cert #53860), 6/22/2022)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: One Location, Prologue
Plot point: Accidental Death, Addiction, Death of a Best Friend, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Depression, Hallucinations, Mountain Climbing
Social setting: Extreme Sports
Subgenre: Survival Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Tea Shop Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

For best friends Becky and Hunter, life is all about conquering fears and pushing limits. But after they climb 2,000 feet to the top of a remote, abandoned radio tower, they find themselves stranded with no way down. Now Becky and Hunter’s expert climbing skills will be put to the ultimate test as they desperately fight to survive the elements, a lack of supplies, and vertigo-inducing heights.
